goal: Make the allowed cell entry point visually discoverable without changing the pore-only wall collision behavior.
changes: Added CellEntryPoreGuide as a non-colliding visual folder under CellEnvironment_Main.
Added a translucent cyan EntryPoreSoftGlow centered just outside the front pore.
Added 22 irregular neon EntryPoreRingMarker beads around the pore so the allowed opening reads as a biological entry point.
Added nine brighter EntryPoreBeaconMarker spheres as a vertical yellow beacon through the gate.
Extended the EntryGuideBubble trail to eight bubbles spanning outward from the front pore, with connecting beams.
Added a CellEntryPoreLight and low-rate soft-textured EntryPoreParticleMist to make the entry easier to find in the water volume without box-like particles.
Kept the existing TopOpenWithPoreGate collision mode: players can enter through the top opening or the front pore, and the other wall blockers remain collidable.

studio_probe.spawn_note: The player spawn is now submarine-side; this guide remains at the front pore entry.

validation: Deployed BuildMainCellExperience.lua into Roblox Studio through the legacy bridge.
Ran require(builder).build() and confirmed the guide exists with zero collidable BaseParts.
Selected Workspace.CellEnvironment_Main.CellEntryPoreGuide in Studio for immediate inspection.
result: The front pore entry remains visibly marked and non-colliding while preserving the existing non-penetrable cell wall and pore-only entry behavior.
